headline: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
description: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

headline: 'It's pure terror against civilians': In Ukraine, Kherson's residents live under constant threat of Russian drones
France's succession of scorching spells reignites the battle for window shutters in major cities
Panic sweeps across California over proposed billionaire tax
description: The proposal, led by the health workers' union, could go to a vote in November after a successful petition. Tech moguls and moderate Democrats are mobilizing their wealth and networks to try to block it.

headline: France's ambiguous memory of slavery
description: Although the Code Noir was finally officially repealed on May 28, it is important to remember that slavery in the French colonies reached its height precisely as the universalist ideals of the Enlightenment were spreading in France, writes journalist Jérôme Gautheret.

headline: Grieving Deschamps to miss France's match against Norway
description: France head coach Didier Deschamps’ mother died on Tuesday, so he will be returning to France for the funeral, with Guy Stéphan taking over for Friday's final Group I game against Norway. The 2026 World Cup is Deschamps' final tournament in charge of France.

headline: 'A man in shorts is as competent as a man in a suit': Young people push back against office dress codes
description: Some young people push back against traditional dress codes that still prevail in many professional sectors. When temperatures rise, the façade cracks and debates become more heated.

headline: North Korea is equipping its navy with nuclear weapons, Kim Jong Un says

Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
description: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

headline: The (not so) stupid questions to ask during a heatwave in France
description: Can we swim in the Seine? Should I eat meat when it is 40 degrees? Can I ask for a glass of water in any café? Le Monde answers your questions.

headline: Ten years after Brexit, the City of London financial district is stronger than ever
description: When the UK left the EU following the referendum on June 23, 2016, the City of London financial district had to relinquish some of its activities that were focused on the European market. But it has since bounced back, recording strong growth in insurance and fintech.

headline: '10th anniversary of Brexit referendum marks event long thought impossible in the UK: The rise of the far right'
